---
title: "Defining and Overcoming the Limits of Multi-Center Metabolomics: A Computational Blueprint for Data Integration"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    code-fold: true
execute:
  dir: project
---

# Introduction

## Input Data

All required input data lives in the `object/` subfolder. These files are
produced by the upstream pipeline (`1_object_creation.qmd` →
`2_global_analysis.qmd` → `3_peak_feature_calculation_detected.qmd`).

I can also provide them pre-computed.

**RData objects** (chromatographic profiles, from `1_object_creation.qmd`):
`bpc_full.RData`, `bpc_detect.RData`, `tic_full.RData`, `tic_detect.RData`


**CSV files** (from `1_object_creation.qmd` and `3_peak_feature_calculation_detected.qmd`):
`detected_peaks_{afekta,cembio,hmgu,icl}_HE.csv`,
`peak_metrics_detected_all4labs.csv`


**Helper files** (in `5_downstream_analysis/`):
`global_analysis_helpers.R`, `lab_setup_phase1.csv`

## Question and Hypothesis

**Core question:** *What are the computational steps required to make multi-center metabolomics data integration possible, and what are the absolute hardware-imposed limits of the field?*

**Hypothesis:** Raw LC-MS data from different laboratories is too confounded by instrumental noise to be directly integrated. Computational processing can recover biological signal, but instrument chemistry imposes an irreducible reproducibility ceiling.

We benchmark four sequential processing strategies:

1. **Peak-picking** (feature extraction vs. raw profiles)
2. **Local (RT-dependent) normalization** (LOESS vs. global scaling)
3. **Peak shape confirmation** (EIC Pearson similarity for cross-lab anchors)
4. **Hardware ceiling** (same-platform ICC as the achievable upper bound)

Data: 19 standard mixtures (HUMAN Endosome) from 4 labs with harmonized LC (same column, mobile phases, gradient) but different MS platforms (Agilent 6546 Q-ToF, Waters Synapt G2S Q-ToF, Sciex Zeno TOF 7600, Agilent 6545 Q-ToF). No technical replicates; lab identity is confounded with instrument platform.

Baseline finding: **lab identity explains 97–100% of variance in raw TIC**
(Full Signal).

## Load Libraries

```{r library, include=TRUE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
## this analysisis dependent on R 4.6.0 and Bioc 3.23
library(Chromatograms)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(here)
library(patchwork)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(pheatmap)
library(readxl)

base_path <- here("5_downstream_analysis")
source(file.path(base_path, "global_analysis_helpers.R"))
```

## Setup Information

```{r load-setup}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 1.0**: Laboratory settings."

setup <- read.csv(file.path(base_path, "lab_setup_phase1.csv"))

setup_simplified <- setup |>
  filter(details == "harmonized") |>
  select(lab, `LC.Instrument`, `MS.Instrument`,
         `Inj.vol..uL..pos`, `Inj.vol..uL..neg`) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(
    platform = case_when(
      grepl("Agilent 1290", `LC.Instrument`) ~ "Agilent",
      grepl("Acquity", `LC.Instrument`) ~ "Waters",
      grepl("ExionLC", `LC.Instrument`) ~ "Sciex",
      grepl("Bruker", `LC.Instrument`) ~ "Bruker",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    ms_instrument = case_when(
      grepl("6546", `MS.Instrument`) ~ "Agilent_6546",
      grepl("6545", `MS.Instrument`) ~ "Agilent_6545",
      grepl("Synapt", `MS.Instrument`) ~ "Waters_Synapt",
      grepl("Zeno", `MS.Instrument`) ~ "Sciex_Zeno",
      grepl("TIMS", `MS.Instrument`) ~ "Bruker_TIMS",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    inj_vol_pos = `Inj.vol..uL..pos`,
    inj_vol_neg = `Inj.vol..uL..neg`
  ) |>
  select(lab, platform, ms_instrument, inj_vol_pos, inj_vol_neg)

setup_simplified
```

## Compute Global Metrics (Raw Signal)

TIC and BPC per chromatogram across data levels and polarities; no normalization (added in Part 2).

```{r compute-metrics}
compute_metrics <- function(polarity, polarity_name, data_level = "full") {
  if (data_level == "full") {
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/bpc_full.RData"))
    bpc <- bpc_full[bpc_full$polarity == polarity]
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_full.RData"))
    tic <- tic_full[tic_full$polarity == polarity]
  } else {
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/bpc_detect.RData"))
    bpc <- bpc_detect[bpc_detect$polarity == polarity]
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_detect.RData"))
    tic <- tic_detect[tic_detect$polarity == polarity]
  }

  data.frame(
    lab = bpc$lab,
    mixture = bpc$mixture,
    polarity = polarity_name,
    bpc = vapply(intensity(bpc), sum, numeric(1), na.rm = TRUE),
    tic = vapply(intensity(tic), sum, numeric(1), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
    left_join(setup_simplified, by = "lab") |>
    mutate(data_level = ifelse(data_level == "full",
                               "Full Signal", "Detected Signal"))
}

metrics_full <- run_for_polarities(compute_metrics, "full")
metrics_detect <- run_for_polarities(compute_metrics, "detect")

# Create Consensus Signal data level from pk_metrics
compute_consensus_metrics <- function(pol, polarity_name, data_level = "detect") {
  pk_metrics <- read.csv(file.path(base_path,
                                   "object/peak_metrics_detected_all4labs.csv"))

  pk_metrics |>
    filter(as.integer(polarity) == pol) |>
    group_by(lab, mixture) |>
    summarise(
      tic = sum(areaUnderTic, na.rm = TRUE),
      bpc = sum(maxIntensity, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    left_join(setup_simplified, by = "lab") |>
    mutate(
      polarity = polarity_name,
      data_level = "Consensus Signal"
    )
}

metrics_consensus <- run_for_polarities(compute_consensus_metrics, "detect")

metrics_combined <- bind_rows(metrics_full, metrics_detect, metrics_consensus)
```

## Data Processing Levels

- **Full Signal**: raw chromatographic profiles (all ions + baseline).
- **Detected Signal**: centWave-picked peaks, per lab (not yet matched).
- **Consensus Signal**: peaks matched across all 4 labs by m/z and RT.

Progression Full → Detected → Consensus tests whether computational processing
progressively removes technical variance.

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="50%", eval = FALSE}
#| fig-cap: "**Graphical Abstract**: Progressive refinement of the TIC from Full Signal to Detected Signal to Consensus Signal."
knitr::include_graphics("C:/Users/amart/Desktop/HUMAN Ring Trial/HUMAN_Ring_Trial-main/HUMAN_Ring_Trial-main/5_downstream_analysis/FullSignal_DetectedSignal_ConsensusSignal.png")
```

---

# Part 1: The Baseline Problem - Feature Extraction is Mandatory {#part1}

**Core claim:** Raw chromatographic profiles cannot be integrated across labs because vendor-specific baseline noise dominates the signal. Feature extraction (peak-picking) is the mandatory first computational step to make data integration theoretically possible.

## 1.1 The Problem: Lab Variance Dominates Raw Data

### 1.1.1 Variance Decomposition

#### 1.1.1.1 Raw TIC

```{r variance-decomposition}
results_table <- data.frame()

for (pol in c("Positive", "Negative")) {
  for (level in c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")) {
    data_subset <- metrics_combined |>
      filter(polarity == pol, data_level == level)
    result <- check_metric_quality(data_subset, "tic")
    results_table <- rbind(results_table, data.frame(
      Polarity = pol, Data_Level = level, R_squared = result$r_squared,
      Lab_Variance_Pct = result$variance_table$Percentage[1],
      Mixture_Variance_Pct = result$variance_table$Percentage[2],
      Residual_Variance_Pct = result$variance_table$Percentage[3]
    ))
  }
}
```

```{r variance-stacked-bar}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.1.1**: Variance decomposition of TIC across all conditions."

variance_long <- results_table |>
  select(Polarity, Data_Level, R_squared, ends_with("_Pct")) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("_Pct"),
               names_to = "Source", values_to = "Percentage") |>
  mutate(
    Source = gsub("_Variance_Pct|_Pct", "", Source),
    Data_Level = factor(Data_Level,
                        levels = c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")),
    Label = paste(Polarity, Data_Level, sep = "\n"),
    Label = factor(Label, levels = unique(paste(
      rep(c("Positive", "Negative"), each = 3),
      rep(c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal"), times = 2),
      sep = "\n"
    ))),
    R2_label = paste("R² =", sprintf("%.3f", R_squared))
  )

text_labels <- variance_long |> distinct(Label, R2_label)

ggplot(variance_long, aes(x = Label, y = Percentage, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = Label, label = R2_label, y = 105),
            data = text_labels, size = 3, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Variance Decomposition: TIC Across All Conditions",
       subtitle = "Lab variance (orange) dominates raw signal — the core problem",
       x = "Polarity & Data Level", y = "Variance (%)", fill = "Source") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  ylim(0, 110)
```
#### 1.1.1.2 BPC

```{r}
results_table <- data.frame()

for (pol in c("Positive", "Negative")) {
  for (level in c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")) {
    data_subset <- metrics_combined |>
      filter(polarity == pol, data_level == level)
    result <- check_metric_quality(data_subset, "bpc")
    results_table <- rbind(results_table, data.frame(
      Polarity = pol, Data_Level = level, R_squared = result$r_squared,
      Lab_Variance_Pct = result$variance_table$Percentage[1],
      Mixture_Variance_Pct = result$variance_table$Percentage[2],
      Residual_Variance_Pct = result$variance_table$Percentage[3]
    ))
  }
}
```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.1.2**: Variance decomposition of BPC across all conditions."

variance_long <- results_table |>
  select(Polarity, Data_Level, R_squared, ends_with("_Pct")) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("_Pct"),
               names_to = "Source", values_to = "Percentage") |>
  mutate(
    Source = gsub("_Variance_Pct|_Pct", "", Source),
    Data_Level = factor(Data_Level,
                        levels = c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")),
    Label = paste(Polarity, Data_Level, sep = "\n"),
    Label = factor(Label, levels = unique(paste(
      rep(c("Positive", "Negative"), each = 3),
      rep(c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal"), times = 2),
      sep = "\n"
    ))),
    R2_label = paste("R² =", sprintf("%.3f", R_squared))
  )

text_labels <- variance_long |> distinct(Label, R2_label)

ggplot(variance_long, aes(x = Label, y = Percentage, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = Label, label = R2_label, y = 105),
            data = text_labels, size = 3, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Variance Decomposition: BPC Across All Conditions",
       subtitle = "Lab variance (orange) dominates raw signal — the core problem",
       x = "Polarity & Data Level", y = "Variance (%)", fill = "Source") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  ylim(0, 110)
```

### 1.1.2 Lab Effect Coefficients

#### 1.1.2.1 Raw TIC

```{r lab-coefficients}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.2.1**: Comparison of Laboratory Effect Coefficients against Afekta (TIC)."

all_coefficients <- data.frame()

for (pol in c("Positive", "Negative")) {
  for (level in c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")) {
    data_subset <- metrics_combined |>
      filter(polarity == pol, data_level == level)
    result <- check_metric_quality(data_subset, "tic")
    all_coefficients <- rbind(all_coefficients,
      result$coefficients |> mutate(Polarity = pol, Data_Level = level))
  }
}

lab_coefs <- all_coefficients |>
  filter(type == "Lab Effect") |>
  mutate(lab_name = gsub("lab", "", coefficient))

lab_coefs <- lab_coefs |>
  mutate(
    Polarity = factor(Polarity, levels = c("Positive", "Negative"))
  )

ggplot(lab_coefs, aes(x = lab_name, y = estimate, fill = lab_name)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  facet_grid(Data_Level ~ Polarity) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Lab Effect Coefficients (deviation from Afekta)",
       x = "Polarity & Laboratory", y = "Coefficient (log2 scale)", fill = "Lab") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
#### 1.1.2.2 BPC

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.2.2**: Comparison of Laboratory Effect Coefficients against Afekta (BPC)."

all_coefficients <- data.frame()

for (pol in c("Positive", "Negative")) {
  for (level in c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")) {
    data_subset <- metrics_combined |>
      filter(polarity == pol, data_level == level)
    result <- check_metric_quality(data_subset, "bpc")
    all_coefficients <- rbind(all_coefficients,
      result$coefficients |> mutate(Polarity = pol, Data_Level = level))
  }
}

lab_coefs <- all_coefficients |>
  filter(type == "Lab Effect") |>
  mutate(lab_name = gsub("lab", "", coefficient))

lab_coefs <- lab_coefs |>
  mutate(
    Polarity = factor(Polarity, levels = c("Positive", "Negative"))
  )

ggplot(lab_coefs, aes(x = lab_name, y = estimate, fill = lab_name)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  facet_grid(Data_Level ~ Polarity) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title = "Lab Effect Coefficients (deviation from Afekta)",
       x = "Polarity & Laboratory", y = "Coefficient (log2 scale)", fill = "Lab") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### 1.1.3 PCA Analysis of Lab vs. Mixture Clustering

Colour = mixture, shape = lab.
Same shape clustering → lab dominates; same colour clustering → mixture (biology) dominates.

#### 1.1.3.1 Raw TIC

```{r pca-setup-tic}
# Prepare data for PCA: wide format with samples as rows, features as columns
# For raw data: use individual RT bins as features
# For detected data: use detected peaks as features

# Function to prepare PCA data
prepare_pca_data <- function(data_level = "full", polarity = "Positive") {
  if (data_level == "full") {
    # Load full signal data
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_full.RData"))
    chr_data <- tic_full[tic_full$polarity == ifelse(polarity == "Positive", 1, 0)]
  } else {
    # Load detected signal data
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_detect.RData"))
    chr_data <- tic_detect[tic_detect$polarity == ifelse(polarity == "Positive", 1, 0)]
  }

  # Extract intensity matrices
  # Each row = one sample, columns = RT bins
  int_list <- intensity(chr_data)
  rt_list <- rtime(chr_data)

  # Create common RT grid
  all_rts <- unlist(rt_list)
  rt_grid <- seq(min(all_rts, na.rm = TRUE),
                 max(all_rts, na.rm = TRUE),
                 length.out = 500)

  # Interpolate each chromatogram onto the grid
  int_matrix <- t(sapply(seq_along(int_list), function(i) {
    approx(rt_list[[i]], int_list[[i]], xout = rt_grid,
           method = "linear", rule = 2)$y
  }))

  # Add sample metadata
  rownames(int_matrix) <- paste0(chr_data$lab, "_", chr_data$mixture)
  colnames(int_matrix) <- paste0("RT_", round(rt_grid, 1))

  # Create metadata data frame
  meta_df <- data.frame(
    sample = rownames(int_matrix),
    lab = chr_data$lab,
    mixture = chr_data$mixture,
    row.names = rownames(int_matrix)
  )

  list(data = int_matrix, meta = meta_df)
}

# Prepare datasets for both polarities
pca_full_pos <- prepare_pca_data("full", "Positive")
pca_full_neg <- prepare_pca_data("full", "Negative")
pca_detect_pos <- prepare_pca_data("detect", "Positive")
pca_detect_neg <- prepare_pca_data("detect", "Negative")

# Prepare consensus data from pk_metrics (wide format: samples x consensus peaks)
# Filter to one polarity at a time — consensus_ids are disjoint across polarities
# and the two modes have different intensity scales.
pk_metrics <- read.csv(file.path(base_path,
                                 "object/peak_metrics_detected_all4labs.csv"))

prepare_pca_consensus <- function(polarity_int) {
  wide <- pk_metrics |>
    filter(as.integer(polarity) == polarity_int) |>
    mutate(sample = paste0(lab, "_", mixture)) |>
    select(sample, consensus_id, areaUnderTic, lab, mixture) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = consensus_id, values_from = areaUnderTic,
                values_fill = 0, names_prefix = "peak_") |>
    arrange(sample)

  data_mat <- wide |> select(starts_with("peak_")) |> as.matrix()
  rownames(data_mat) <- wide$sample

  list(data = data_mat, meta = wide |> select(sample, lab, mixture))
}

pca_consensus_pos <- prepare_pca_consensus(1L)
pca_consensus_neg <- prepare_pca_consensus(0L)

# Helper: run prcomp and return a scores data frame tagged with polarity.
# `transform_fn` applies a transform before PCA (identity for continuous TIC
# profiles, log1p for sparse consensus peaks).
pca_scores_df <- function(pca_list, polarity_label, transform_fn = identity) {
  res <- prcomp(transform_fn(pca_list$data), scale. = TRUE, center = TRUE)
  var_expl <- (res$sdev^2 / sum(res$sdev^2)) * 100
  list(
    scores = data.frame(
      PC1 = res$x[, 1], PC2 = res$x[, 2],
      lab = pca_list$meta$lab, mixture = pca_list$meta$mixture,
      polarity = polarity_label
    ),
    var_expl = var_expl
  )
}

# Common plot: 2-panel PCA (Positive | Negative).
# Colour encodes mixture (biological signal) and shape encodes lab (technical).
# As data level progresses Full → Detected → Consensus, colours should tighten
# (mixture clusters emerge) while shapes should disperse within each colour
# (lab effects shrink).
plot_pca_by_polarity <- function(pos, neg, title, subtitle) {
  scores <- bind_rows(pos$scores, neg$scores) |>
    mutate(polarity = factor(polarity, levels = c("Positive", "Negative")))
  panel_labels <- c(
    Positive = sprintf("Positive  (PC1 %.1f%%, PC2 %.1f%%)",
                       pos$var_expl[1], pos$var_expl[2]),
    Negative = sprintf("Negative  (PC1 %.1f%%, PC2 %.1f%%)",
                       neg$var_expl[1], neg$var_expl[2])
  )
  ggplot(scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = mixture, shape = lab)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85, stroke = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~polarity, scales = "free",
               labeller = labeller(polarity = panel_labels)) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
    labs(title = title, subtitle = subtitle,
         x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Mixture", shape = "Laboratory") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}
```

```{r pca-raw-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.3.1.1**: PCA of Raw TIC profiles, Positive and Negative polarity. Colour = mixture, shape = lab. Same-shape points cluster (labs group together); colours are scattered — technical noise dominates biological signal."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

plot_pca_by_polarity(
  pos = pca_scores_df(pca_full_pos, "Positive"),
  neg = pca_scores_df(pca_full_neg, "Negative"),
  title = "PCA: Raw TIC Profiles (Full Signal)",
  subtitle = "Shapes cluster (lab dominates), colours scattered (mixture signal buried)"
)
```

```{r pca-detected-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.3.1.2**: PCA of Detected Peaks, Positive and Negative polarity. Colour = mixture, shape = lab. Lab clusters weaken and same-colour points start to group together — feature extraction recovers biological signal."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

plot_pca_by_polarity(
  pos = pca_scores_df(pca_detect_pos, "Positive"),
  neg = pca_scores_df(pca_detect_neg, "Negative"),
  title = "PCA: Detected Peaks (Peak-Picked Signal)",
  subtitle = "Shapes disperse, colours begin to group — mixture signal emerges"
)
```

```{r pca-consensus-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.3.1.3**: PCA of Consensus Peaks, Positive and Negative polarity. Colour = mixture, shape = lab. Same-colour points cluster tightly regardless of shape — mixture dominates, lab effects minimized. log1p compresses the intensity range because each peak has ~4 non-zero values (labs) and ~72 zeros (other mixtures); without it the presence/absence gap dominates scaling."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

plot_pca_by_polarity(
  pos = pca_scores_df(pca_consensus_pos, "Positive", transform_fn = log1p),
  neg = pca_scores_df(pca_consensus_neg, "Negative", transform_fn = log1p),
  title = "PCA: Consensus Peaks (Cross-Lab Matched Signal)",
  subtitle = "Colours cluster by mixture across all shapes — biology dominates technical variance"
)
```

```{r consensus-matrix-example}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 1.1**: Example structure of the consensus peak matrix."
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

# data_pca_consensus_pos <- pca_consensus_neg[["data"]]
# data_pca_consensus_pos <- as.data.frame(data_pca_consensus_pos)

library(tibble)
library(knitr)

# Example structure of the consensus peak matrix
# Rows = samples (lab + mixture)
# Columns = consensus peaks
# Most values are zeros because each consensus peak
# only exists in one biological mixture.

example_consensus_matrix <- tribble(
  ~sample,        ~peak_1, ~peak_2, ~peak_3,
  "LabA_mix1",      10000,        0,        0,
  "LabB_mix1",       9000,        0,        0,
  "LabC_mix1",      11000,        0,        0,
  "LabD_mix1",      12000,        0,        0,
  "LabA_mix2",          0,     7000,        0,
  "LabB_mix2",          0,     6800,        0,
  "LabC_mix2",          0,     7200,        0,
  "LabD_mix2",          0,     7100,        0
)

kable(
  example_consensus_matrix,
  caption = paste0(
    "<span style='white-space: nowrap;'><b>Example structure of the consensus peak matrix.</b></span>",
    "<br>",
    "Each consensus peak is only present within one mixture (across laboratories), producing many zeros."
  ),
  align = "lccc",
  escape = FALSE
)
```

#### 1.1.3.2 BPC

```{r pca-setup-bpc}
# Prepare data for PCA: wide format with samples as rows, features as columns
# For raw data: use individual RT bins as features
# For detected data: use detected peaks as features

# Function to prepare PCA data
prepare_pca_data <- function(data_level = "full", polarity = "Positive") {
  if (data_level == "full") {
    # Load full signal data
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/bpc_full.RData"))
    chr_data <- bpc_full[bpc_full$polarity == ifelse(polarity == "Positive", 1, 0)]
  } else {
    # Load detected signal data
    load(file.path(base_path, "object/bpc_detect.RData"))
    chr_data <- bpc_detect[bpc_detect$polarity == ifelse(polarity == "Positive", 1, 0)]
  }

  # Extract intensity matrices
  # Each row = one sample, columns = RT bins
  int_list <- intensity(chr_data)
  rt_list <- rtime(chr_data)

  # Create common RT grid
  all_rts <- unlist(rt_list)
  rt_grid <- seq(min(all_rts, na.rm = TRUE),
                 max(all_rts, na.rm = TRUE),
                 length.out = 500)

  # Interpolate each chromatogram onto the grid
  int_matrix <- t(sapply(seq_along(int_list), function(i) {
    approx(rt_list[[i]], int_list[[i]], xout = rt_grid,
           method = "linear", rule = 2)$y
  }))

  # Add sample metadata
  rownames(int_matrix) <- paste0(chr_data$lab, "_", chr_data$mixture)
  colnames(int_matrix) <- paste0("RT_", round(rt_grid, 1))

  # Create metadata data frame
  meta_df <- data.frame(
    sample = rownames(int_matrix),
    lab = chr_data$lab,
    mixture = chr_data$mixture,
    row.names = rownames(int_matrix)
  )

  list(data = int_matrix, meta = meta_df)
}

# Prepare datasets for both polarities
pca_full_pos <- prepare_pca_data("full", "Positive")
pca_full_neg <- prepare_pca_data("full", "Negative")
pca_detect_pos <- prepare_pca_data("detect", "Positive")
pca_detect_neg <- prepare_pca_data("detect", "Negative")

# Prepare consensus data from pk_metrics (wide format: samples x consensus peaks)
# Filter to one polarity at a time — consensus_ids are disjoint across polarities
# and the two modes have different intensity scales.
pk_metrics <- read.csv(file.path(base_path,
                                 "object/peak_metrics_detected_all4labs.csv"))

prepare_pca_consensus <- function(polarity_int) {
  wide <- pk_metrics |>
    filter(as.integer(polarity) == polarity_int) |>
    mutate(sample = paste0(lab, "_", mixture)) |>
    select(sample, consensus_id, areaUnderTic, lab, mixture) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = consensus_id, values_from = areaUnderTic,
                values_fill = 0, names_prefix = "peak_") |>
    arrange(sample)

  data_mat <- wide |> select(starts_with("peak_")) |> as.matrix()
  rownames(data_mat) <- wide$sample

  list(data = data_mat, meta = wide |> select(sample, lab, mixture))
}

pca_consensus_pos <- prepare_pca_consensus(1L)
pca_consensus_neg <- prepare_pca_consensus(0L)

# Helper: run prcomp and return a scores data frame tagged with polarity.
# `transform_fn` applies a transform before PCA (identity for continuous TIC
# profiles, log1p for sparse consensus peaks).
pca_scores_df <- function(pca_list, polarity_label, transform_fn = identity) {
  res <- prcomp(transform_fn(pca_list$data), scale. = TRUE, center = TRUE)
  var_expl <- (res$sdev^2 / sum(res$sdev^2)) * 100
  list(
    scores = data.frame(
      PC1 = res$x[, 1], PC2 = res$x[, 2],
      lab = pca_list$meta$lab, mixture = pca_list$meta$mixture,
      polarity = polarity_label
    ),
    var_expl = var_expl
  )
}

# Common plot: 2-panel PCA (Positive | Negative).
# Colour encodes mixture (biological signal) and shape encodes lab (technical).
# As data level progresses Full → Detected → Consensus, colours should tighten
# (mixture clusters emerge) while shapes should disperse within each colour
# (lab effects shrink).
plot_pca_by_polarity <- function(pos, neg, title, subtitle) {
  scores <- bind_rows(pos$scores, neg$scores) |>
    mutate(polarity = factor(polarity, levels = c("Positive", "Negative")))
  panel_labels <- c(
    Positive = sprintf("Positive  (PC1 %.1f%%, PC2 %.1f%%)",
                       pos$var_expl[1], pos$var_expl[2]),
    Negative = sprintf("Negative  (PC1 %.1f%%, PC2 %.1f%%)",
                       neg$var_expl[1], neg$var_expl[2])
  )
  ggplot(scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = mixture, shape = lab)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85, stroke = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~polarity, scales = "free",
               labeller = labeller(polarity = panel_labels)) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
    labs(title = title, subtitle = subtitle,
         x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Mixture", shape = "Laboratory") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}
```

```{r pca-raw-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.3.2.1**: PCA of BPC profiles, Positive and Negative polarity. Colour = mixture, shape = lab. Same-shape points cluster (labs group together); colours are scattered — technical noise dominates biological signal."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

plot_pca_by_polarity(
  pos = pca_scores_df(pca_full_pos, "Positive"),
  neg = pca_scores_df(pca_full_neg, "Negative"),
  title = "PCA: BPC Profiles (Full Signal)",
  subtitle = "Shapes cluster (lab dominates), colours scattered (mixture signal buried)"
)
```

```{r pca-detected-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.3.2.2**: PCA of Detected Peaks, Positive and Negative polarity. Colour = mixture, shape = lab. Lab clusters weaken and same-colour points start to group together — feature extraction recovers biological signal."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

plot_pca_by_polarity(
  pos = pca_scores_df(pca_detect_pos, "Positive"),
  neg = pca_scores_df(pca_detect_neg, "Negative"),
  title = "PCA: Detected Peaks (Peak-Picked Signal)",
  subtitle = "Shapes disperse, colours begin to group — mixture signal emerges"
)
```

```{r pca-consensus-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.1.3.2.3**: PCA of Consensus Peaks, Positive and Negative polarity. Colour = mixture, shape = lab. Same-colour points cluster tightly regardless of shape — mixture dominates, lab effects minimized. log1p compresses the intensity range because each peak has ~4 non-zero values (labs) and ~72 zeros (other mixtures); without it the presence/absence gap dominates scaling."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

plot_pca_by_polarity(
  pos = pca_scores_df(pca_consensus_pos, "Positive", transform_fn = log1p),
  neg = pca_scores_df(pca_consensus_neg, "Negative", transform_fn = log1p),
  title = "PCA: Consensus Peaks (Cross-Lab Matched Signal)",
  subtitle = "Colours cluster by mixture across all shapes — biology dominates technical variance"
)
```

**Conclusion:** Raw profile data cannot be integrated across labs;
feature extraction followed by cross-lab matching is required.

## 1.2 ICC: Full Signal vs Detected Signal

### 1.2.1 ICC Computation

ICC quantifies the ratio of biological signal to total variance:

$$ICC = \frac{\sigma^2_{between}}{\sigma^2_{between} + \sigma^2_{within}} \tag{Equation 1}$$

- $\sigma^2_{between}$ = variance *between* mixtures (biological signal)
- $\sigma^2_{within}$ = variance *within* the same mixture across labs (technical noise)
- ICC > 0.5 → acceptable reproducibility

```{r icc-computation}
# Raw signal ICC only — normalization strategies introduced in Part 2
icc_all_results <- data.frame()

for (pol in c("Positive", "Negative")) {
  for (level in c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")) {
    data_subset <- metrics_combined |>
      filter(polarity == pol, data_level == level)

    results <- compute_icc(data_subset, "tic")

    if (!all(is.na(data_subset$bpc))) {
      results <- bind_rows(
        results,
        compute_icc(data_subset, "bpc")
      )
    }

    results <- results |>
      mutate(Polarity = pol, Data_Level = level,
             Metric_Label = case_when(
               Metric == "tic" ~ "TIC",
               Metric == "bpc" ~ "BPC",
               TRUE ~ Metric))

    icc_all_results <- bind_rows(icc_all_results, results)
  }
}
```

### 1.2.2 Peak Detection Impact

#### 1.2.2.1 Raw TIC

```{r peak-detection-impact-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 1.2.2.1**: ICC improvement from peak detection (raw TIC) in both positive and negative mode."

icc_comparison_tic <- icc_all_results |>
  filter(Metric == "tic") |>
  select(Polarity, Data_Level, ICC) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Data_Level, values_from = ICC)

knitr::kable(icc_comparison_tic,
             caption = "ICC improvement from peak detection (raw TIC)",
             digits = 3)
```

```{r peak-detection-plot-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.2.2.1**: Raw TIC ICC across data levels. Peak detection improves ICC on raw TIC. Consensus Signal (matched peaks) achieves ICC = 0.436, far above Full and Detected Signal levels. Consensus peaks reveal the true signal ceiling. Peak matching is the critical bottleneck, not peak detection alone."

default_cols <- scales::hue_pal()(3)

custom_cols <- c(
  "Full Signal" = default_cols[3],
  "Detected Signal" = default_cols[2],
  "Consensus Signal" = default_cols[1]
)

ggplot(icc_all_results |>
         filter(Metric == "tic") |>
         mutate(Data_Level = factor(Data_Level,
                                    levels = c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")),
                Polarity = factor(Polarity,
                                  levels = c("Positive", "Negative"))
                ),
       aes(x = Polarity, y = ICC, fill = Data_Level)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", ICC)),
            position = position_dodge(0.9), vjust = -0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_cols) +
  labs(title = "Impact of Peak Detection on Reproducibility",
       subtitle = "Raw TIC (no normalization); dashed = 0.5 threshold",
       y = "ICC", x = "Polarity", fill = "Processing Level") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  ylim(0, 1)
```

#### 1.2.2.2 BPC

```{r peak-detection-impact-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 1.2.2.2**: ICC improvement from peak detection (BPC) in both positive and negative mode."

icc_comparison_tic <- icc_all_results |>
  filter(Metric == "bpc") |>
  select(Polarity, Data_Level, ICC) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Data_Level, values_from = ICC)

knitr::kable(icc_comparison_tic,
             caption = "ICC improvement from peak detection (BPC)",
             digits = 3)
```

```{r peak-detection-plot-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.2.2.2**: BPC ICC across data levels. Peak detection improves ICC on BPC. Consensus Signal (matched peaks) achieves ICC = 0.441, far above Full and Detected Signal levels. Consensus peaks reveal the true signal ceiling. Peak matching is the critical bottleneck, not peak detection alone."

default_cols <- scales::hue_pal()(3)

custom_cols <- c(
  "Full Signal" = default_cols[3],
  "Detected Signal" = default_cols[2],
  "Consensus Signal" = default_cols[1]
)

ggplot(icc_all_results |>
         filter(Metric == "bpc") |>
         mutate(Data_Level = factor(Data_Level,
                                    levels = c("Full Signal", "Detected Signal", "Consensus Signal")),
                Polarity = factor(Polarity,
                                  levels = c("Positive", "Negative"))
                ),
       aes(x = Polarity, y = ICC, fill = Data_Level)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", ICC)),
            position = position_dodge(0.9), vjust = -0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_cols) +
  labs(title = "Impact of Peak Detection on Reproducibility",
       subtitle = "BPC (no normalization); dashed = 0.5 threshold",
       y = "ICC", x = "Polarity", fill = "Processing Level") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  ylim(0, 1)
```

### 1.2.3 ICC Variance Decomposition

```{r icc-variance-decomposition}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1.3.3**: Peak detection shifts variance toward biological signal (raw signal, no normalization)."

variance_plot <- icc_all_results |>
  select(Polarity, Data_Level, Metric_Label,
         Between_mixture_var, Within_mixture_var) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Between_mixture_var, Within_mixture_var),
               names_to = "Source", values_to = "Variance") |>
  mutate(Source = case_when(
    Source == "Between_mixture_var" ~ "Between mixtures (signal)",
    Source == "Within_mixture_var" ~ "Within mixtures (lab noise)"
  ))

variance_plot <- variance_plot |>
  mutate(
    Polarity = factor(Polarity, levels = c("Positive", "Negative"))
  )

ggplot(variance_plot,
       aes(x = Metric_Label, y = Variance, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(Data_Level ~ Polarity, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Between mixtures (signal)" = "#619CFF",
    "Within mixtures (lab noise)" = "#F8766D")) +
  labs(title = "Variance Decomposition: Raw TIC and BPC",
       x = "Polarity & Metric", y = "Variance (log2 scale)", fill = "Source") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```
---

# Part 2: Addressing RT-Dependent Bias Makes Patient Pooling Possible {#strategy2}

**Core claim:** RT-dependent drift between labs makes patient pooling
statistically invalid without local normalization.

**Data scope:** Consensus Signal — cross-lab matched peaks (peaks present
in all 4 labs), at per-peak Area Under TIC granularity.

## 2.0 Add Normalization Variants

Median-scaled and autoscaled variants of TIC/BPC, per (lab, polarity, data_level).

```{r add-normalization-variants}
metrics_combined <- metrics_combined |>
  group_by(lab, polarity, data_level) |>
  mutate(
    bpc_median_scaled = bpc / median(bpc, na.rm = TRUE),
    tic_median_scaled = tic / median(tic, na.rm = TRUE),
    bpc_autoscaled = (bpc - mean(bpc, na.rm = TRUE)) / mad(bpc, na.rm = TRUE),
    tic_autoscaled = (tic - mean(tic, na.rm = TRUE)) / mad(tic, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup()
```

## 2.1 Normalization Impact

### 2.1.0 Why Choose Median Scaling vs. Autoscaling vs. No Normalization?

- **Median scaling**: removes systematic per-lab sensitivity differences;
  appropriate when the design is balanced (all labs run the same 19 mixtures).
- **Autoscaling** (dividing by SD/MAD): amplifies low-abundance noise.
- **No normalization**: baseline — lab-to-lab intensity differences remain.

### 2.1.1 Global Normalization: Median Scaling

#### 2.1.1.1 Raw TIC

```{r normalization-variance-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.1.1.1**: Effect of normalization on variance decomposition (TIC)."

test_data <- metrics_combined |>
  filter(polarity == "Positive", data_level == "Consensus Signal")

result_raw <- check_metric_quality(test_data, "tic")
result_median <- check_metric_quality(test_data, "tic_median_scaled")
result_auto <- check_metric_quality(test_data, "tic_autoscaled")

normalization_results <- bind_rows(
  result_raw$variance_table |>
    mutate(Normalization = "Raw TIC", R_squared = result_raw$r_squared),
  result_median$variance_table |>
    mutate(Normalization = "Median Scaled", R_squared = result_median$r_squared),
  result_auto$variance_table |>
    mutate(Normalization = "Autoscaled (MAD)", R_squared = result_auto$r_squared)
)

normalization_results |>
  mutate(Normalization = factor(Normalization,
    levels = c("Raw TIC", "Median Scaled", "Autoscaled (MAD)")
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Normalization, y = Percentage, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Normalization on Variance Decomposition (TIC)",
    subtitle = "Positive Mode, Consensus Signal",
    x = "Normalization Method", y = "Variance (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```

#### 2.1.1.2 BPC

```{r normalization-variance-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.1.1.2**: Effect of normalization on variance decomposition (BPC)."

test_data <- metrics_combined |>
  filter(polarity == "Positive", data_level == "Consensus Signal")

result_raw <- check_metric_quality(test_data, "bpc")
result_median <- check_metric_quality(test_data, "bpc_median_scaled")
result_auto <- check_metric_quality(test_data, "bpc_autoscaled")

normalization_results <- bind_rows(
  result_raw$variance_table |>
    mutate(Normalization = "BPC", R_squared = result_raw$r_squared),
  result_median$variance_table |>
    mutate(Normalization = "Median Scaled", R_squared = result_median$r_squared),
  result_auto$variance_table |>
    mutate(Normalization = "Autoscaled (MAD)", R_squared = result_auto$r_squared)
)

normalization_results |>
  mutate(Normalization = factor(Normalization,
    levels = c("BPC", "Median Scaled", "Autoscaled (MAD)")
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Normalization, y = Percentage, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Normalization on Variance Decomposition (BPC)",
    subtitle = "Positive Mode, Consensus Signal",
    x = "Normalization Method", y = "Variance (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
```

### 2.1.2 Residual Comparison

#### 2.1.2.1 Raw TIC

```{r normalization-residuals-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.1.2.1**: Effect of normalization on residual distribution (TIC)."
#| fig-height: 16

mod_raw <- lm(log2(tic) ~ lab + mixture, data = test_data)
mod_median <- lm(log2(tic_median_scaled) ~ lab + mixture,
                 data = test_data)
mod_auto <- lm(tic_autoscaled ~ lab + mixture,
               data = test_data) # Autoscaling often produces negative values centered around 0, and this is incompatible with log2()

(
  plot_residuals_new(mod_raw, "Raw TIC (log2)", test_data) /
  plot_residuals_new(mod_median, "Median Scaled TIC", test_data) /
  plot_residuals_new(mod_auto, "Autoscaled TIC (MAD)", test_data)
) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Residual Plots: Raw vs Median vs Autoscaled",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))
  )
```

#### 2.1.2.2 BPC

```{r normalization-residuals-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.1.2.2**: Effect of normalization on residual distribution (BPC)."
#| fig-height: 16

mod_raw <- lm(log2(bpc) ~ lab + mixture, data = test_data)
mod_median <- lm(log2(bpc_median_scaled) ~ lab + mixture,
                 data = test_data)
mod_auto <- lm(bpc_autoscaled ~ lab + mixture,
               data = test_data) # Autoscaling often produces negative values centered around 0, and this is incompatible with log2()

(
  plot_residuals_new(mod_raw, "BPC (log2)", test_data) /
  plot_residuals_new(mod_median, "Median Scaled BPC", test_data) /
  plot_residuals_new(mod_auto, "Autoscaled BPC (MAD)", test_data)
) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Residual Plots: Raw vs Median vs Autoscaled",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))
  )
```

## 2.2 The Problem: Non-Linear Sensitivity Profiles

### 2.2.1 Instrument Sensitivity Across RT Quartiles

Non-parallel lines across quartiles → RT-dependent biases that global
normalization cannot fix.

#### 2.2.1.1 Raw TIC

```{r sensitivity-profile-tic}
compute_consortium_sensitivity <- function(polarity) {
  load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_detect.RData"))
  tic_data <- tic_detect[tic_detect$polarity == polarity]
  breaks <- quantile(unlist(rtime(tic_data)),
                     probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE)

  raw_stats <- t(vapply(seq_along(tic_data), function(i) {
    rt <- rtime(tic_data[i])[[1]]
    int <- intensity(tic_data[i])[[1]]
    vapply(1:4, function(q) {
      sum(int[rt >= breaks[q] & rt < breaks[q + 1]], na.rm = TRUE)
    }, numeric(1))
  }, numeric(4)))

  df <- data.frame(
    lab = tic_data$lab, mixture = tic_data$mixture,
    Q1 = raw_stats[, 1], Q2 = raw_stats[, 2],
    Q3 = raw_stats[, 3], Q4 = raw_stats[, 4]
  )
  grand_medians <- df |>
    summarise(across(starts_with("Q"), \(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Quartile", values_to = "Consensus")

  df |>
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Q"),
                 names_to = "Quartile", values_to = "Intensity") |>
    left_join(grand_medians, by = "Quartile") |>
    mutate(
      Log2_Deviation = log2(Intensity / Consensus),
      Polarity = ifelse(polarity == 1, "Positive", "Negative")
    )
}

all_profiles <- run_for_polarities_simple(compute_consortium_sensitivity)
```

```{r sensitivity-profile-plot-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.2.1.1**: Non-parallel sensitivity profiles → global normalization insufficient (TIC)."

all_profiles |>
  mutate(Log2_TIC = log2(Intensity)) |>
  group_by(Quartile, lab, Polarity) |>
  summarise(median_log2_tic = median(Log2_TIC, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Quartile, y = median_log2_tic,
             group = lab, color = lab)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Polarity) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "Raw TIC Distribution Across RT Quartiles",
    subtitle = "Non-parallel lines → global normalization is insufficient",
    y = "log2(TIC per Quartile)", x = "Quartile", color = "Laboratory"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r sensitivity-deviation-table-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 2.2.1.1**: Consortium median comparison by lab and RT quartile (TIC)"

findings_table <- all_profiles |>
  mutate(Log2_Dev = log2(Intensity / Consensus)) |>
  group_by(lab, Quartile, Polarity) |>
  summarise(Median_Log2_Dev = round(median(Log2_Dev, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Quartile, values_from = Median_Log2_Dev)

knitr::kable(findings_table,
             caption = "Median log2 deviation from consortium median by lab and RT quartile")
```

ICL: Q1 +0.24 → Q4 −0.78 (positive). Cembio: Q3 +1.55 log2 deviation (negative).

#### 2.2.1.2 BPC

```{r sensitivity-profile-bpc}
compute_consortium_sensitivity <- function(polarity) {
  load(file.path(base_path, "object/bpc_detect.RData"))
  bpc_data <- bpc_detect[bpc_detect$polarity == polarity]
  breaks <- quantile(unlist(rtime(bpc_data)),
                     probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE)

  raw_stats <- t(vapply(seq_along(bpc_data), function(i) {
    rt <- rtime(bpc_data[i])[[1]]
    int <- intensity(bpc_data[i])[[1]]
    vapply(1:4, function(q) {
      sum(int[rt >= breaks[q] & rt < breaks[q + 1]], na.rm = TRUE)
    }, numeric(1))
  }, numeric(4)))

  df <- data.frame(
    lab = bpc_data$lab, mixture = bpc_data$mixture,
    Q1 = raw_stats[, 1], Q2 = raw_stats[, 2],
    Q3 = raw_stats[, 3], Q4 = raw_stats[, 4]
  )
  grand_medians <- df |>
    summarise(across(starts_with("Q"), \(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Quartile", values_to = "Consensus")

  df |>
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Q"),
                 names_to = "Quartile", values_to = "Intensity") |>
    left_join(grand_medians, by = "Quartile") |>
    mutate(
      Log2_Deviation = log2(Intensity / Consensus),
      Polarity = ifelse(polarity == 1, "Positive", "Negative")
    )
}

all_profiles <- run_for_polarities_simple(compute_consortium_sensitivity)
```

```{r sensitivity-profile-plot-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.2.1.2**: Non-parallel sensitivity profiles → global normalization insufficient (BPC)."

all_profiles |>
  mutate(Log2_BPC = log2(Intensity)) |>
  group_by(Quartile, lab, Polarity) |>
  summarise(median_log2_bpc = median(Log2_BPC, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Quartile, y = median_log2_bpc,
             group = lab, color = lab)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Polarity) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "BPC Distribution Across RT Quartiles",
    subtitle = "Non-parallel lines → global normalization is insufficient",
    y = "log2(BPC per Quartile)", x = "Quartile", color = "Laboratory"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r sensitivity-deviation-table-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 2.2.1.2**: Consortium median comparison by lab and RT quartile (BPC)"

findings_table <- all_profiles |>
  mutate(Log2_Dev = log2(Intensity / Consensus)) |>
  group_by(lab, Quartile, Polarity) |>
  summarise(Median_Log2_Dev = round(median(Log2_Dev, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Quartile, values_from = Median_Log2_Dev)

knitr::kable(findings_table,
             caption = "Median log2 deviation from consortium median by lab and RT quartile")
```

### 2.2.2 Residual Lab Variance by RT Quartile

```{r quartile-variance}
compute_rt_quartiles_biological <- function(polarity, polarity_name) {
  load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_detect.RData"))
  tic_data <- tic_detect[tic_detect$polarity == polarity]
  load(file.path(base_path, "object/bpc_detect.RData"))
  bpc_data <- bpc_detect[bpc_detect$polarity == polarity]

  tic_medians <- data.frame(
    lab = tic_data$lab,
    tic_total = vapply(intensity(tic_data), sum, numeric(1), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
    group_by(lab) |>
    summarise(median_tic = median(tic_total, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  bpc_medians <- data.frame(
    lab = bpc_data$lab,
    bpc_total = vapply(intensity(bpc_data), sum, numeric(1), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
    group_by(lab) |>
    summarise(median_bpc = median(bpc_total, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  all_rts <- unlist(rtime(tic_data))
  if (length(all_rts) == 0) return(NULL)
  global_breaks <- quantile(all_rts, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE)

  compute_quartile_sums <- function(int_vec, rt_vec, breaks, lab_median) {
    vapply(1:4, function(q) {
      sum(int_vec[rt_vec >= breaks[q] & rt_vec < breaks[q + 1]],
          na.rm = TRUE) / lab_median
    }, numeric(1))
  }

  stats <- t(vapply(seq_along(tic_data), function(i) {
    rt <- rtime(tic_data[i])[[1]]
    c(compute_quartile_sums(
        intensity(tic_data[i])[[1]], rt, global_breaks,
        tic_medians$median_tic[tic_medians$lab == tic_data$lab[i]]),
      compute_quartile_sums(
        intensity(bpc_data[i])[[1]], rt, global_breaks,
        bpc_medians$median_bpc[bpc_medians$lab == bpc_data$lab[i]]))
  }, numeric(8)))

  colnames(stats) <- c(paste0("TIC_Frac_Q", 1:4), paste0("BPC_Frac_Q", 1:4))

  data.frame(
    lab = tic_data$lab, mixture = tic_data$mixture,
    polarity = polarity_name, stats
  )
}

bio_combined_quartiles <- run_for_polarities_names(
  compute_rt_quartiles_biological
)

quartile_results <- expand.grid(
  Polarity = c("Positive", "Negative"),
  Metric = c(paste0("TIC_Frac_Q", 1:4), paste0("BPC_Frac_Q", 1:4))
) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(var_result = list(
    get_variance(filter(bio_combined_quartiles, polarity == Polarity), Metric)
  )) |>
  unnest(var_result)
```

#### 2.2.2.1 Raw TIC

```{r quartile-variance-plot-tic}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.2.2.1**: TIC Residual lab variance peaks in Q4 (~53% positive mode, ~40% negative mode) after global median normalization, with Q3 also elevated (~29–34%)."

plot_quartile_variance(quartile_results, "TIC")
```

#### 2.2.2.2 BPC

```{r quartile-variance-plot-bpc}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.2.2.2**: BPC Residual lab variance peaks in Q4 (~53% positive mode, ~40% negative mode) after global median normalization, with Q3 also elevated (~29–34%)."

plot_quartile_variance(quartile_results, "BPC")
```

## 2.3 Comparing Normalization Strategies

**Question:** can normalization push ICC above the post-detection baseline
(raw TIC ICC ≈ 0.024–0.059), and can RT-dependent normalization fix
quartile-specific biases?

Three granularities compared (per-peak Area Under TIC):

1. **Global Median** — one scaling factor per lab
2. **Decile Median** — per-RT-decile scaling factors (10 bins)
3. **LOESS** — continuous RT-dependent normalization

### 2.3.1 Prepare Peak-Level Data

```{r load-peak-data}
pk_metrics <- read.csv(file.path(base_path,
                                 "object/peak_metrics_detected_all4labs.csv"))

cat("Peak metrics:", nrow(pk_metrics), "rows\n")
cat("Unique consensus peaks:", n_distinct(pk_metrics$consensus_id), "\n")
```

### 2.3.2 Apply Normalization Strategies

```{r normalization-strategies}
# Load RT breaks from detected signal (deciles instead of quartiles)
load(file.path(base_path, "object/tic_detect.RData"))
all_rts <- unlist(rtime(tic_detect))
rt_breaks_decile <- quantile(all_rts, probs = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1), na.rm = TRUE)

# Load peak tables with RT info
pt_all_labs <- bind_rows(
  read.csv(file.path(base_path, "object/detected_peaks_afekta_HE.csv")) |>
    mutate(lab = "afekta"),
  read.csv(file.path(base_path, "object/detected_peaks_hmgu_HE.csv")) |>
    mutate(lab = "hmgu"),
  read.csv(file.path(base_path, "object/detected_peaks_icl_HE.csv")) |>
    mutate(lab = "icl"),
  read.csv(file.path(base_path, "object/detected_peaks_cembio_HE.csv")) |>
    mutate(lab = "cembio")
)

# Add RT and decile assignment (10 bins: D1 to D10)
pk_norm <- pk_metrics |>
  left_join(
    pt_all_labs |> select(chrom_peak_id, rt, lab),
    by = c("chrom_peak_id", "lab")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    rt_decile = cut(rt,
                    breaks = rt_breaks_decile,
                    labels = paste0("D", 1:10),
                    include.lowest = TRUE)
  )

# Positive and negative ionization have very different intensity distributions,
# so all three strategies compute normalization factors per (lab, polarity) —
# pooling modes would bias each one's scaling factor.

# --- Strategy 1: Global Median ---
global_medians <- pk_norm |>
  group_by(lab, polarity) |>
  summarise(
    global_median_area = median(areaUnderTic, na.rm = TRUE),
    global_median_max = median(maxIntensity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

grand_medians <- global_medians |>
  group_by(polarity) |>
  summarise(
    grand_median_area = median(global_median_area),
    grand_median_max = median(global_median_max),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

pk_norm <- pk_norm |>
  left_join(global_medians, by = c("lab", "polarity")) |>
  left_join(grand_medians, by = "polarity") |>
  mutate(
    area_global = areaUnderTic / global_median_area * grand_median_area,
    max_global = maxIntensity / global_median_max * grand_median_max
  )

# --- Strategy 2: Decile Median (NEW) ---
decile_medians <- pk_norm |>
  filter(!is.na(rt_decile)) |>
  group_by(lab, polarity, rt_decile) |>
  summarise(
    d_median_area = median(areaUnderTic, na.rm = TRUE),
    d_median_max = median(maxIntensity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

grand_d_medians <- decile_medians |>
  group_by(polarity, rt_decile) |>
  summarise(
    grand_d_area = median(d_median_area),
    grand_d_max = median(d_median_max),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

pk_norm <- pk_norm |>
  left_join(decile_medians, by = c("lab", "polarity", "rt_decile")) |>
  left_join(grand_d_medians, by = c("polarity", "rt_decile")) |>
  mutate(
    area_decile = areaUnderTic / d_median_area * grand_d_area,
    max_decile = maxIntensity / d_median_max * grand_d_max
  )

# --- Strategy 3: LOESS ---
pk_norm <- pk_norm |>
  group_by(lab, polarity) |>
  mutate(
    loess_area = {
      fit <- loess(log2(areaUnderTic) ~ rt, span = 0.5,
                   na.action = na.exclude)
      predict(fit)
    },
    loess_max = {
      fit <- loess(log2(maxIntensity) ~ rt, span = 0.5,
                   na.action = na.exclude)
      predict(fit)
    }
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Grand LOESS (consensus curve across all labs, fit separately per polarity)
pk_norm <- pk_norm |>
  group_by(polarity) |>
  mutate(
    grand_loess_area = {
      fit <- loess(log2(areaUnderTic) ~ rt, span = 0.5,
                   na.action = na.exclude)
      predict(fit)
    },
    grand_loess_max = {
      fit <- loess(log2(maxIntensity) ~ rt, span = 0.5,
                   na.action = na.exclude)
      predict(fit)
    }
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    area_loess = 2^(log2(areaUnderTic) - loess_area + grand_loess_area),
    max_loess = 2^(log2(maxIntensity) - loess_max + grand_loess_max)
  )

cat("Normalization complete. Rows:", nrow(pk_norm), "\n")
```

### 2.3.3 LOESS

#### 2.3.3.1 Visualize LOESS Sensitivity Profiles

```{r loess-profiles}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.3.1**: Per-lab LOESS sensitivity curves showing the RT-dependent bias that local normalization corrects."

pk_norm |>
  filter(!is.na(rt), !is.na(loess_area)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = rt, y = loess_area, color = lab)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(polarity, labels = c("Negative", "Positive"))) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "LOESS Sensitivity Profiles by Lab",
    subtitle = "log2(Area) smoothed over RT — divergence = what local norm corrects",
    x = "Retention Time (s)", y = "LOESS-smoothed log2(Area)",
    color = "Laboratory"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### 2.3.3.2 Log2 Fold-Error: Proof of LOESS Correction

Per-peak Log2 Fold-Error $= \log_2(\text{Lab Area} / \text{Consensus Median Area})$.
Before normalization, labs drift from zero (especially Q3/Q4). After LOESS,
all labs re-center on zero if the systematic bias is corrected.

```{r log2-fold-error-calculation}
# Calculate consensus median area for each peak (across all labs)
consensus_areas <- pk_norm |>
  group_by(consensus_id, rt_decile) |>
  summarise(
    consensus_median_raw = median(areaUnderTic, na.rm = TRUE),
    consensus_median_global = median(area_global, na.rm = TRUE),
    consensus_median_decile = median(area_decile, na.rm = TRUE),
    consensus_median_loess = median(area_loess, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Calculate log2 fold-error for each lab
fold_error <- pk_norm |>
  left_join(consensus_areas, by = c("consensus_id", "rt_decile")) |>
  mutate(
    log2_fe_raw = log2(areaUnderTic / consensus_median_raw),
    log2_fe_global = log2(area_global / consensus_median_global),
    log2_fe_decile = log2(area_decile / consensus_median_decile),
    log2_fe_loess = log2(area_loess / consensus_median_loess)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(rt_decile), !is.na(log2_fe_raw)) |>
  select(lab, consensus_id, rt_decile, log2_fe_raw, log2_fe_global, log2_fe_decile, log2_fe_loess)

cat("Log2 Fold-Error dataset:", nrow(fold_error), "rows\n")
```

### 2.3.4 Log2 Fold-Error Visualization

```{r log2-fold-error-plot}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.4**: Log2 Fold-Error by RT Decile and Lab, showing the effect of LOESS normalization. Before correction (Raw, left), labs drift far from zero in D7-D10 (ICL systematically low, Cembio hyper-sensitive in D8). After Global Median (middle), drift persists. After LOESS (right), all labs are perfectly re-centered on zero across all deciles—the proof that systematic RT-dependent bias is corrected."

fe_long <- fold_error |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("log2_fe_"),
    names_to = "strategy",
    names_prefix = "log2_fe_",
    values_to = "log2_fold_error"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    strategy = case_when(
      strategy == "raw" ~ "Raw Area",
      strategy == "global" ~ "Global Median",
      strategy == "decile" ~ "Decile Median",
      strategy == "loess" ~ "LOESS"
    ),
    strategy = factor(strategy, levels = c("Raw Area", "Global Median", "Decile Median", "LOESS"))
  )

ggplot(fe_long, aes(x = rt_decile, y = log2_fold_error, fill = lab)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~strategy) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "Log2 Fold-Error: Raw vs Normalized Areas",
    subtitle = "LOESS re-centers all labs on zero (systematic bias correction)",
    x = "RT Decile", y = "log2(Lab Area / Consensus Median)", fill = "Lab"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

**Findings**:
- **Raw Area**: ICL D1 +0.45, D4 +0.64, D5 −0.56, D7 −0.60, D8 −0.68;
  Cembio D8 +0.52, D10 −1.26.
- **Global Median**: drift persists (RT-specific bias unaddressed).
- **LOESS**: all labs re-centered on zero across deciles.

### 2.3.5 ICC by Normalization Strategy

ICC computed on **peak-level Area Under TIC** after each normalization
strategy, per RT decile, on the **Consensus Signal** (peaks matched in
all 4 labs).

#### 2.3.5.1 By RT Decile

```{r icc-peak-function}
compute_icc_peak <- function(data, value_col) {
  wide <- data |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[value_col]])) |>
    select(consensus_id, lab, value = all_of(value_col)) |>
    mutate(value = log2(value)) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = lab, values_from = value)

  # Filter to only rows with all 4 labs present
  # Get actual lab columns (may not always be all 4)
  lab_cols <- intersect(c("afekta", "hmgu", "icl", "cembio"), colnames(wide))
  wide <- wide |> filter(if_all(all_of(lab_cols), ~ !is.na(.x)))

  if (nrow(wide) < 10) {
    return(data.frame(
      Metric = value_col, ICC = NA_real_,
      Between_var = NA_real_, Within_var = NA_real_, n_peaks = nrow(wide)))
  }

  mat <- as.matrix(wide[, lab_cols])
  row_means <- rowMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  grand_mean <- mean(row_means, na.rm = TRUE)

  k <- ncol(mat); n <- nrow(mat)
  ss_between <- k * sum((row_means - grand_mean)^2)
  ss_within <- sum(sweep(mat, 1, row_means)^2, na.rm = TRUE)

  ms_between <- ss_between / (n - 1)
  ms_within <- ss_within / (n * (k - 1))
  icc_val <- (ms_between - ms_within) / (ms_between + (k - 1) * ms_within)

  data.frame(
    Metric = value_col, ICC = icc_val,
    Between_var = ms_between, Within_var = ms_within, n_peaks = n
  )
}
```

```{r icc-normalization-comparison}
# Consensus Signal: peaks matched across all 4 labs
consensus_signal <- pk_norm |>
  filter(!is.na(rt_decile)) |>
  group_by(consensus_id) |>
  filter(n() == 4) |>
  ungroup()

# Compute ICC per RT decile for each strategy
icc_by_decile <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:10, function(d) {
  pk_consensus <- consensus_signal |> filter(rt_decile == paste0("D", d))

  bind_rows(
    compute_icc_peak(pk_consensus, "areaUnderTic") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "Raw", Decile = paste0("D", d)),
    compute_icc_peak(pk_consensus, "area_global") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "Global Median", Decile = paste0("D", d)),
    compute_icc_peak(pk_consensus, "area_decile") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "Decile Median", Decile = paste0("D", d)),
    compute_icc_peak(pk_consensus, "area_loess") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "LOESS", Decile = paste0("D", d))
  )
}))

# Also compute overall ICC for comparison
icc_overall <- bind_rows(
  compute_icc_peak(consensus_signal, "areaUnderTic") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "Raw", Decile = "Overall"),
  compute_icc_peak(consensus_signal, "area_global") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "Global Median", Decile = "Overall"),
  compute_icc_peak(consensus_signal, "area_decile") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "Decile Median", Decile = "Overall"),
  compute_icc_peak(consensus_signal, "area_loess") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "LOESS", Decile = "Overall")
)

icc_norm_results <- bind_rows(icc_by_decile, icc_overall)

# Summary table: decile-specific improvements
icc_table_consensus <- icc_norm_results |>
  filter(Decile != "Overall") |>
  select(Strategy, Decile, ICC) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Decile, values_from = ICC) |>
  select(Strategy, D1, D2, D3, D4, D5, D6, D7, D8, D9, D10)

knitr::kable(icc_table_consensus,
    caption = "Table 2.3.5.1: ICC by normalization strategy and RT decile",
    digits = 3)
```

**Key Findings**:

1. Overall peak-level ICC (consensus peaks): **0.33 raw → 0.55 Global → 0.58 Decile → 0.58 LOESS**.
2. Local-normalization gains concentrated in D7–D10.
3. Decile Median ≈ LOESS (0.58 vs 0.58) but simpler.

```{r icc-normalization-line-plot}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.5.1**: ICC by Normalization Strategy and RT Decile (Consensus Signal). Local normalization (Decile Median, LOESS) improves ICC most in late deciles (D7–D10) where RT-dependent bias is strongest."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

icc_plot_data <- icc_norm_results |>
  filter(Decile != "Overall", !is.na(ICC)) |>
  mutate(
    Decile_num = as.numeric(gsub("D", "", Decile)),
    Strategy = factor(Strategy, levels = c("Raw", "Global Median", "Decile Median", "LOESS"))
  )

ggplot(icc_plot_data, aes(x = Decile_num, y = ICC, color = Strategy, linetype = Strategy)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10, labels = paste0("D", 1:10)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Raw" = "#E64B35", "Global Median" = "#4DBBD5",
                                 "Decile Median" = "#70AD47", "LOESS" = "#4472C4")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Raw" = "solid", "Global Median" = "solid",
                                    "Decile Median" = "solid", "LOESS" = "dashed")) +
  labs(
    title = "ICC by Normalization Strategy and RT Decile",
    subtitle = "Local normalization (Decile Median, LOESS) improves ICC most in later deciles (D7–D10) where RT-dependent bias is strongest",
    x = "RT Decile", y = "ICC (Area Under TIC)", color = "Strategy", linetype = "Strategy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  ylim(NA, 1)
```

#### 2.3.5.2 Global ICC across all RT

```{r icc-overall-comparison}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.5.2**: Overall ICC comparison across strategies (Consensus Signal, aggregated across all deciles)."

icc_norm_results |>
  filter(Decile == "Overall") |>
  mutate(Strategy = factor(Strategy, levels = c("Raw", "Global Median",
                                                 "Decile Median", "LOESS"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Strategy, y = ICC, fill = Strategy)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(ICC, 3)), vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Raw" = "#E64B35", "Global Median" = "#4DBBD5",
                               "Decile Median" = "#70AD47", "LOESS" = "#4472C4")) +
  labs(
    title = "Overall ICC by Normalization Strategy",
    subtitle = "Consensus Signal — Area Under TIC; dashed line at ICC = 0.5 threshold",
    y = "ICC", x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) +
  ylim(NA, 1)
```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 2.3.5.2**: Global ICC across all RT per strategy"

icc_global_table <- icc_norm_results |>
  filter(Decile == "Overall") |>
  select(Strategy, ICC, Between_var, Within_var, n_peaks)

knitr::kable(
  icc_global_table,
  caption = "Table 2.3.5.2: Global ICC across all RT values",
  digits = 3
)
```

#### 2.3.5.3 Pairwise ICC: Afekta vs Cembio

This analysis evaluates reproducibility between only two laboratories (Afekta and Cembio), allowing direct assessment of pairwise agreement after normalization.

```{r icc-afekta-cembio}
compute_icc_peak_pair <- function(data, value_col,
                                  labs_keep = c("afekta", "cembio")) {

  wide <- data |>
    filter(lab %in% labs_keep) |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[value_col]])) |>
    select(consensus_id, lab, value = all_of(value_col)) |>
    mutate(value = log2(value)) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from = lab, values_from = value)

  # Keep only peaks present in both labs
  wide <- wide |>
    filter(if_all(all_of(labs_keep), ~ !is.na(.x)))

  if (nrow(wide) < 10) {
    return(data.frame(
      Metric = value_col,
      ICC = NA_real_,
      Between_var = NA_real_,
      Within_var = NA_real_,
      n_peaks = nrow(wide)
    ))
  }

  mat <- as.matrix(wide[, labs_keep])

  row_means <- rowMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  grand_mean <- mean(row_means, na.rm = TRUE)

  k <- ncol(mat)
  n <- nrow(mat)

  ss_between <- k * sum((row_means - grand_mean)^2)
  ss_within <- sum(sweep(mat, 1, row_means)^2, na.rm = TRUE)

  ms_between <- ss_between / (n - 1)
  ms_within <- ss_within / (n * (k - 1))

  icc_val <- (ms_between - ms_within) /
    (ms_between + (k - 1) * ms_within)

  data.frame(
    Metric = value_col,
    ICC = icc_val,
    Between_var = ms_between,
    Within_var = ms_within,
    n_peaks = n
  )
}

# Prepare data
pair_signal <- pk_norm |>
  filter(!is.na(rt_decile)) |>
  filter(lab %in% c("afekta", "cembio"))

# ICC by RT decile
icc_pair_by_decile <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:10, function(d) {

  pk_dec <- pair_signal |>
    filter(rt_decile == paste0("D", d))

  bind_rows(
    compute_icc_peak_pair(pk_dec, "areaUnderTic") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "Raw", Decile = paste0("D", d)),

    compute_icc_peak_pair(pk_dec, "area_global") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "Global Median", Decile = paste0("D", d)),

    compute_icc_peak_pair(pk_dec, "area_decile") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "Decile Median", Decile = paste0("D", d)),

    compute_icc_peak_pair(pk_dec, "area_loess") |>
      mutate(Strategy = "LOESS", Decile = paste0("D", d))
  )
}))

# Overall ICC across all RT
icc_pair_overall <- bind_rows(

  compute_icc_peak_pair(pair_signal, "areaUnderTic") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "Raw", Decile = "Overall"),

  compute_icc_peak_pair(pair_signal, "area_global") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "Global Median", Decile = "Overall"),

  compute_icc_peak_pair(pair_signal, "area_decile") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "Decile Median", Decile = "Overall"),

  compute_icc_peak_pair(pair_signal, "area_loess") |>
    mutate(Strategy = "LOESS", Decile = "Overall")
)

icc_pair_results <- bind_rows(
  icc_pair_by_decile,
  icc_pair_overall
)
```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.5.3.1**: Pairwise ICC between Afekta and Cembio across RT deciles."

icc_pair_plot <- icc_pair_results |>
  filter(Decile != "Overall") |>
  mutate(
    Decile_num = as.numeric(gsub("D", "", Decile)),
    Strategy = factor(
      Strategy,
      levels = c("Raw", "Global Median",
                 "Decile Median", "LOESS")
    )
  )

ggplot(
  icc_pair_plot,
  aes(x = Decile_num, y = ICC,
      color = Strategy, linetype = Strategy)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.5,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "gray50"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:10,
    labels = paste0("D", 1:10)
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Raw" = "#E64B35",
    "Global Median" = "#4DBBD5",
    "Decile Median" = "#70AD47",
    "LOESS" = "#4472C4"
  )) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(
    "Raw" = "solid",
    "Global Median" = "solid",
    "Decile Median" = "solid",
    "LOESS" = "dashed"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Pairwise ICC: Afekta vs Cembio",
    subtitle = "Local normalization improves pairwise reproducibility in late RT regions",
    x = "RT Decile",
    y = "ICC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  ylim(NA, 1)
```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.5.3.2**: Overall pairwise ICC between Afekta and Cembio across normalization strategies."

icc_pair_results |>
  filter(Decile == "Overall") |>
  mutate(
    Strategy = factor(
      Strategy,
      levels = c("Raw", "Global Median",
                 "Decile Median", "LOESS")
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Strategy, y = ICC, fill = Strategy)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.5,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", ICC)),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Raw" = "#E64B35",
    "Global Median" = "#4DBBD5",
    "Decile Median" = "#70AD47",
    "LOESS" = "#4472C4"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Overall Pairwise ICC: Afekta vs Cembio",
    subtitle = "ICC aggregated across all RT values",
    x = NULL,
    y = "ICC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  ylim(0, 1)
```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "**Table 2.3.5.3.1**: Global pairwise ICC between Afekta and Cembio."

icc_pair_results |>
  filter(Decile == "Overall") |>
  select(Strategy, ICC, Between_var, Within_var, n_peaks) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "Table 2.3.5.3.1: Pairwise ICC across all RT values",
    digits = 3
  )
```

### 2.3.6 CV by RT Decile and Strategy

```{r cv-by-strategy}
cv_strategies <- pk_norm |>
  filter(!is.na(rt_decile)) |>
  group_by(consensus_id) |>
  filter(n() == 4) |>
  summarise(
    cv_raw = sd(areaUnderTic, na.rm = TRUE) /
      mean(areaUnderTic, na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_global = sd(area_global, na.rm = TRUE) /
      mean(area_global, na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_decile = sd(area_decile, na.rm = TRUE) /
      mean(area_decile, na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_loess = sd(area_loess, na.rm = TRUE) /
      mean(area_loess, na.rm = TRUE),
    rt_decile = first(rt_decile),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cv_strat_long <- cv_strategies |>
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("cv_"),
               names_to = "Strategy", values_to = "CV",
               names_prefix = "cv_") |>
  mutate(Strategy = case_when(
    Strategy == "raw" ~ "Raw",
    Strategy == "global" ~ "Global Median",
    Strategy == "decile" ~ "Decile Median",
    Strategy == "loess" ~ "LOESS"
  ))

cv_strat_long |>
  group_by(Strategy) |>
  summarise(Median_CV = median(CV, na.rm = TRUE),
            Mean_CV = mean(CV, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "CV summary by normalization strategy", digits = 3)
```

```{r cv-quartile-strategy-plot}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2.3.6**: CV by RT Decile and strategy. Global, Decile, and LOESS normalization show similar performance across all deciles."

cv_strat_summary <- cv_strat_long |>
  filter(!is.na(CV), CV < 2) |>
  group_by(Strategy, rt_decile) |>
  summarise(Median_CV = median(CV, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(cv_strat_summary, aes(x = rt_decile, y = Median_CV, fill = Strategy)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Median_CV, 2)),
            position = position_dodge(0.9), vjust = -0.3, size = 2.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Median Inter-Lab CV by RT Decile and Normalization Strategy",
    subtitle = "Global vs local normalization effects across retention time bins",
    x = "RT Decile", y = "CV (Area Under TIC)", fill = "Strategy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

Median inter-lab CV: 0.97 raw → 0.82–0.84 normalized.

**Open question for Part 3:** LOESS removes systematic RT-dependent bias
(Figure 2.3) but ICC gains are modest. Hypothesis: residual integration noise
(baseline boundaries, scan-to-scan fluctuation) sets the ICC ceiling, not
systematic bias. Part 3 tests this by replacing scalar integration metrics
with EIC profile similarity.

---

# Part 3: Residual Cross-Lab Variance — Quality, Intensity, RT, Shape {#strategy3}

After peak picking (Part 1) and RT-dependent normalisation (Part 2),
how much lab variance remains on consensus peaks, and where does it
live? We partition it into four observable axes:

- **Per-lab peak quality** (§3.3): what each lab contributes to
  consensus, density per metric.
- **Inter-lab relative abundance** (§3.4): scale-invariant intensity
  agreement, lab-pair Pearson r on `log2(AUC / lab median)`.
- **Cross-lab metric correlations** (§3.5): same-platform vs
  cross-platform gap per scalar metric.
- **Retention-time drift** (§3.6): the floor on RT alignment after
  normalisation, and its RT-dependence across the gradient.

The same-platform pair afekta:cembio (Agilent Q-TOF) acts throughout
as an internal reference for what same-platform agreement looks like —
the practical ceiling for cross-lab reproducibility in this trial.
Positive and negative polarities are kept separate.

## 3.1 Load Pre-Processed EICs

EICs are boundary-trimmed and NA-imputed by `3_peak_feature_calculation_detected.qmd`
and saved to `eics_filtered_detected_all4labs.RData` — no further preprocessing needed here.

```{r peak-boundaries-once}
load(file.path(base_path, "object/eics_filtered_detected_all4labs.RData"))
cat("Loaded", length(final_eics), "EICs across",
    length(unique(final_eics$consensus_id)), "consensus peaks\n")
```

## 3.2 Extract Pre-Computed Metrics

Scalar metrics from `MsQuality::calculateMetrics()` are already in
`chromData(final_eics)` (computed in `3_peak_feature_calculation_detected.qmd`).
We pull them, normalise the `polarity` column (`chromExtract` returns it
in different encodings depending on package version), and additionally
record the apex RT per EIC — needed for the RT-drift analysis in 3.4.

```{r extract-metrics}
pk_metrics <- as.data.frame(chromData(final_eics))
stopifnot("polarity" %in% names(pk_metrics))

pol_to_label <- function(x) {
  s <- as.character(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    s %in% c("1", "1L", "Positive", "positive", "pos", "POS", "+") ~ "Positive",
    s %in% c("0", "0L", "Negative", "negative", "neg", "NEG", "-") ~ "Negative",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}
pk_metrics$polarity_label <- pol_to_label(pk_metrics$polarity)
stopifnot(sum(!is.na(pk_metrics$polarity_label)) > 0)

# Apex RT per EIC (= RT of the maximum non-NA intensity). One quick lapply
# over peaksData; needed for 3.4. Not a "new metric" — just locating the
# already-extracted apex on the RT axis.
pk_metrics$apex_rt <- vapply(seq_along(final_eics), function(i) {
  pd <- peaksData(final_eics[i])[[1]]
  ok <- !is.na(pd$intensity)
  if (sum(ok) < 1) return(NA_real_)
  pd$rtime[ok][which.max(pd$intensity[ok])]
}, numeric(1))

cat("Rows:", nrow(pk_metrics),
    "| polarity:",
    paste(names(table(pk_metrics$polarity_label)),
          table(pk_metrics$polarity_label), sep = "=", collapse = ", "),
    "\n")
```

## 3.3 Per-Lab Peak Quality Distributions

What does each lab actually contribute to consensus? Densities for one
intensity metric (`areaUnderTic`, log2-scaled) and four shape/quality
metrics (`peakWidth`, `signalToNoiseRatio`, `xicFwhm`,
`gaussianSimilarity`), split by polarity. Differences in the shape of
these distributions across labs bound how much intensity- or
shape-based normalisation can hope to recover.

```{r quality-distributions, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 3.1**: Per-lab peak-quality metric distributions, by polarity. Each panel has its own x/y range. Filtered consensus peaks only."
keep_metrics <- c("log2(areaUnderTic)", "peakWidth",
                  "signalToNoiseRatio", "xicFwhm", "gaussianSimilarity")

quality_long <- pk_metrics |>
  filter(!is.na(polarity_label)) |>
  transmute(
    lab,
    polarity             = polarity_label,
    `log2(areaUnderTic)` = log2(pmax(areaUnderTic, 1e-9)),
    peakWidth, signalToNoiseRatio, xicFwhm,
    gaussianSimilarity   = `gaussianSimilarity.gaussian_similarity`
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(-c(lab, polarity),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value), is.finite(value)) |>
  mutate(metric = factor(metric, levels = keep_metrics))

ggplot(quality_long, aes(x = value, fill = lab, colour = lab)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(vars(polarity, metric), scales = "free", ncol = 5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(title    = "Peak-quality distributions per lab",
       subtitle = paste0("Consensus peaks retained after filter ",
                         "(n = ", n_distinct(pk_metrics$consensus_id), ")"),
       x = NULL, y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

Intensity scale (`log2(areaUnderTic)`) shifts substantially between
labs — the absolute response is platform- and sensitivity-dependent and
cannot be compared directly without normalisation. Width-like metrics
(`peakWidth`, `xicFwhm`) overlap more across labs, while
`signalToNoiseRatio` and `gaussianSimilarity` show intermediate
agreement: most lab variance lives on the intensity axis, less on the
shape axis.

## 3.4 Inter-Lab Relative Abundance Correlation

Absolute `areaUnderTic` is not directly comparable across labs —
instrument sensitivity, ionisation efficiency and injection volume
shift the whole intensity scale. To make labs comparable, we compute a
log2 fold change of each peak's AUC against its lab's own median AUC,
within each polarity:

`rel_abund[peak, lab] = log2( AUC[peak, lab] / median(AUC[, lab]) )`

The lab median becomes 0, a peak twice as intense as the lab median is
+1, half-median is −1, regardless of the lab's absolute scale. Pearson
r between two labs' relative-abundance columns over the same set of
consensus peaks asks whether peaks that are above-median in lab A are
also above-median in lab B — a direct, scale-invariant ceiling on
intensity reproducibility.

```{r abundance-correlation}
LABS <- c("afekta", "cembio", "hmgu", "icl")
lab_pairs <- combn(LABS, 2, simplify = FALSE)

abund_wide <- pk_metrics |>
  filter(!is.na(polarity_label),
         !is.na(areaUnderTic), areaUnderTic > 0) |>
  group_by(lab, polarity_label) |>
  mutate(rel_abund = log2(areaUnderTic / median(areaUnderTic))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(consensus_id, polarity = polarity_label, lab, rel_abund) |>
  group_by(consensus_id, polarity) |>
  filter(n() == 4) |>
  ungroup() |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = lab, values_from = rel_abund) |>
  drop_na(all_of(LABS))

pair_corrs <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("Positive", "Negative"), function(pol) {
  sub <- abund_wide[abund_wide$polarity == pol, ]
  do.call(rbind, lapply(lab_pairs, function(p) {
    v <- sub[, p]
    data.frame(
      polarity      = pol,
      pair          = paste(p, collapse = ":"),
      same_platform = identical(sort(p), c("afekta", "cembio")),
      r             = cor(v[[1]], v[[2]]),
      n             = nrow(v)
    )
  }))
}))
pair_corrs <- pair_corrs[order(pair_corrs$polarity, -pair_corrs$r), ]

knitr::kable(transform(pair_corrs, r = round(r, 3)),
  caption = "Inter-lab Pearson r on log2(AUC / lab median), by polarity. Consensus peaks present in all 4 labs.")
```

```{r abundance-scatter, fig.width=11, fig.height=8}
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 3.2**: Inter-lab log2(AUC / lab median) per lab pair. Red dashed = identity; black = OLS fit. Same-platform afekta:cembio highlighted."
abund_pairs <- do.call(rbind, lapply(lab_pairs, function(p) {
  data.frame(
    consensus_id  = abund_wide$consensus_id,
    polarity      = abund_wide$polarity,
    x             = abund_wide[[p[1]]],
    y             = abund_wide[[p[2]]],
    pair          = paste(p, collapse = ":"),
```
